categories.valparaisoDesc: The most colorful city in South America + coast and vineyards

why.title: Why choose us
why.item1Desc: Not drivers. Guides who know every corner and can explain why Chile is the way it is.
why.item2Desc: Your group, your pace. No shared vans with strangers.
why.item3Desc: Preferred access to the best wineries in Casablanca, Colchagua and beyond.
why.item4Desc: Spanish, English and Portuguese. Not machine translations — guides who think in your language.

cta.title: Ready to explore Chile?
cta.subtitle: Tell us about your group and we'll design the perfect tour.

testimonials.title: What our travelers say
testimonials.subtitle: Over 100 private groups guided across central Chile

1.text: Sebastián knows every winery like it's his own. He told us the story of Chilean wine while we tasted the best Sauvignon Blancs in Casablanca Valley. An experience you won't find at any agency.
1.name: Karen D.

2.text: Cristóbal spoke Portuguese the entire time and the color of the water at Embalse El Yeso looked unreal — pure turquoise. It felt like exploring Chile with a local friend. 100% recommended.
2.name: Matteo R.

3.text: The glacier hike was breathtaking. Sebastian explained why the glacier is retreating and made us understand the Andes in a way we never expected. This was the highlight of our entire trip to Chile.
3.name: Sarah L.

custom.title: Looking for something more personal?
custom.subtitle: We design your tour from scratch: dates, destinations, duration and activities tailored to your group.
custom.detail: Birthday, honeymoon, friends trip, corporate visit? Tell us and we'll handle everything.

tourDesc.embalse-el-yeso: At 2,475 meters above sea level, the water of El Yeso Reservoir isn't blue or green — it's turquoise. A color that doesn't seem real until you see it with your own eyes. We're going to understand why the Andes hide one of Chile's most breathtaking landscapes.
tourDesc.el-yeso-las-melosas: Two destinations, one day. First the turquoise water of El Yeso Reservoir, then the depth of Las Melosas valley. A full day through the heart of Cajón del Maipo.
tourDesc.el-yeso-termas-colina: We climb to Colina's natural hot springs — one of the few still untouched in the mountains — and on the way back we pass El Yeso Reservoir. Water that heals, water that amazes.
tourDesc.cajon-del-maipo-panoramico: Cajón del Maipo is more than a canyon. It's where the Maipo River is born, where the Mapuche people lived, and where you can still feel that Santiago is far away. A tour connecting history, nature and landscape.
tourDesc.termas-valle-colina: Valle de Colina Hot Springs sit at 2,800 meters, are freely accessible and very few people know they exist. That's exactly the kind of place we look for.
tourDesc.valparaiso-vina-del-mar: Valparaíso is the most photographed city in Chile, but to understand it you need to climb its hills, read its murals and learn what they mean. Then we head down to Viña del Mar's coast to finish with sea and sun.
tourDesc.valparaiso-vinedo: Valparaíso in the morning, a winery in Casablanca in the afternoon. We combine the most colorful city in Chile with one of the coldest and most elegant wine valleys in the country.
tourDesc.isla-negra-undurraga: Pablo Neruda's house museum at Isla Negra is one of the most personal and surprising places in Chile. We then continue to Viña Undurraga — one of the oldest wineries in the country — to understand how the Chilean wine industry was born.
tourDesc.valparaiso-casas-del-bosque: Valparaíso in the morning, Casablanca Valley in the afternoon with a visit to Casas del Bosque — a boutique winery with one of the best vineyard-view terraces in the region.
tourDesc.andes-panoramico: Santiago has a 6,000-meter mountain as a permanent backdrop. On this tour we drive up to Farellones to get closer to that mountain and understand why the Andes are the Andes.
tourDesc.city-tour-santiago: Santiago has more layers than it seems. Plaza de Armas, Barrio Italia, Cerro Santa Lucía, markets and viewpoints. A tour that puts one of Latin America's most dynamic capitals in context.
tourDesc.tour-casablanca: We're not going on a wine tour. We're going to understand why Chilean wine stopped being a secret. The Casablanca Valley has microclimates that make improbable things possible — Sauvignon Blanc and Pinot Noir meters from the Pacific Ocean.
tourDesc.tour-premium: The best wineries, the finest wines, an experience designed for those who want the highest Chile has to offer in wine tourism. Access to wineries that normally require reservations weeks in advance.
tourDesc.tour-colchagua: Colchagua Valley produces the world's most recognized Carménère. A 3-hour journey from Santiago worth every kilometer — monumental wineries, vineyard landscapes and the colonial town of Santa Cruz.
tourDesc.tour-santa-rita: Santa Rita is not just a winery. It's a 19th-century estate with a museum, century-old gardens and wines that have won international awards for decades. A tour that blends Chilean history with wine culture.
tourDesc.tour-santiago-wines: Santiago's closest wineries — Cousiño Macul and Undurraga — have been producing wine for over 100 years. A half-day tour that makes it clear why Chile was a pioneer in viticulture.
tourDesc.sunset-alyan: Sunset, wine and silence. An intimate experience designed for small groups who want to end the day in the best possible way.
tourDesc.mirador-condores: The Andean condor has a 3-meter wingspan. Watching it soar over the Andes from a natural viewpoint is one of those experiences you never forget.
tourDesc.glaciar-el-morado: El Morado Glacier is retreating. You can still reach it, still see it, still understand what water means in the Andes. A trek that is also a geography lesson.
tourDesc.laguna-del-inca: On the border with Argentina, at 2,950 meters, lies one of the most beautiful lagoons in the Andes. The water changes color with the light — blue, green, turquoise. And the story of the Inca resting at its bottom is real.
tourDesc.parque-la-campana: Darwin climbed this hill in 1834 and wrote that it was one of the most beautiful places he'd ever seen. The Chilean palm forest is still there, and so is the view from the summit.
tourDesc.lagunillas-rafting: Morning trekking at Lagunillas, afternoon rafting on the Maipo River. A full day for those who want both mountain and water.
tourDesc.rafting-asado: Class III-IV rafting on the Maipo River, followed by a traditional Chilean asado on the riverbank. Adrenaline and culture in one tour.
tourDesc.cabalgata-lo-barnechea: At the foot of the Andes, minutes from Santiago, a horseback ride along trails that lead straight to the mountains. No prior experience needed.
tourDesc.parque-farellones: Farellones has zip lines, mountain biking and mountain activities for all levels. A day at altitude with the Andes as your backdrop.

about.title: Turismo CaraCara
about.subtitle: We make it happen
about.story: We are a private tour operator based in Santiago, specialized in central Chile. We were born in 2023 with a simple idea: to make tourists not just see Chile, but understand it.
about.story2: Today we operate with expert guides who speak Spanish, English and Portuguese, and we have agreements with 14 of the best wineries in the country. Every tour is private — your group, your pace.
about.mission: To connect travelers with the history, culture and nature of Chile through authentic experiences, guided by people who know the territory and know how to share it.
about.sebastianDesc: Founder of CaraCara and the driving force behind every operation. He knows every Andean route, every winery and every curve in Cajón del Maipo.
about.cristobalDesc: The one who keeps everything organized and also goes out in the field. Multilingual with an eye for the details that make the difference on every tour.
about.fleetDesc: We operate with 2 Hyundai H1 vans (9 passengers each), plus external vehicles coordinated on demand. Private tours with the capacity to scale.
about.conveniosDesc: We have special rates and preferred access at 14 wineries across Chile:
